mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not positive following months with positive PAYEMS growth in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate, specifically an increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, signals labor market slackening that precedes downward earnings revisions and risk-averse portfolio rebalancing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in goods supply and consumer demand that amplifies recession risk beyond either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of INDPRO and RSAFS declines does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or a logistic regression interaction term yields a p-value greater than 0.10 in a walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, the level of the federal funds rate directly sets the risk-free discount rate for equity valuations; higher rates reduce the present value of future cash flows and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, the 60-day forward return following months with FEDFUNDS above the sample median is not significantly lower than the unconditional return (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, which boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not follow positive INDPRO surprises in more than 52% of months, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal over a 2-year rolling window is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail sales exceeding two percent signals a sudden contraction in consumer spending that reduces top-line revenue visibility and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines >2% month-over-month, or the mean conditional return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When rising unemployment coincides with a rising federal funds rate, the joint pressure on household debt service costs and corporate profitability creates a nonlinear drag on equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises over prior 3 months and FEDFUNDS rises over prior 3 months
failure_condition: The joint condition does not generate negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or an interaction term in a linear model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals stronger residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, labor, and credit, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not follow months with rising HOUST in more than 52% of observations, or the information coefficient is below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.
